Headline: Bybit becomes the first crypto exchange fully licensed by UAE's Securities and Commodities Authority (SCA) in July 2026 — expanding Abu Dhabi and Dubai operations with 500+ planned staff, validating USDT infrastructure as fully regulated mainstream in the UAE
Why It Matters: The UAE SCA's full licence for Bybit (world's second-largest crypto exchange) is the strongest institutional signal yet that USDT and crypto infrastructure has regulatorily cleared the highest bar in the UAE — the hub of GWC's MENA Forex broker outreach. UAE Forex brokers that still lack USDT deposit rails now face competitive exposure from a fully-licensed USDT exchange operating in their home market. GWC outreach to UAE-headquartered Forex brokers without USDT deposits should reference the Bybit SCA approval as definitive proof that USDT payment infrastructure has regulatory mainstream status in the UAE — eliminating the most common compliance objection.
Suggested Action: Consider referencing Bybit UAE SCA full licence in outreach to UAE Forex brokers lacking USDT deposit infrastructure — frame as: the UAE's own regulator has now fully licensed a USDT-native exchange, making the absence of USDT deposits a confirmed competitive gap for every UAE broker
